description: Attestation semantics specimens. These are NOT cross-implementation conformance vectors yet — they document what attestation semantic fields mean with executable examples. Only Python evaluates claims today.

specimens.name: claim_pass
specimens.description: Both required receipt types present. All claims satisfied.
specimens.reviewer_interpretation: The evidence contains both a model execution record and a guardian safety check. Both claims in the claim set are satisfied. A reviewer may treat this as sufficient evidence that the model was executed under guardian oversight — subject to trust in the signer, key integrity, and pack mechanical integrity (verified separately).

specimens.name: claim_insufficient
specimens.description: Only model_call receipt present. Guardian verdict missing. One claim fails.

expected.failure_reason: guardian_verdict receipt type not found in evidence

specimens.reviewer_interpretation: The evidence shows a model execution but no guardian safety check. The claim that guardian oversight occurred is NOT supported by the evidence. A reviewer should treat this as a gap in the evidence chain — the model ran, but there is no proof it was checked. This may or may not be acceptable depending on the governance context and required assurance level.

notes.scope: These specimens demonstrate attestation-level semantic interpretation only. They do not define governance rules, proof-tier requirements, or reviewer decision procedures.
notes.claim_evaluation_runtime: Python only (claim_verifier.py). TS verifier does not evaluate claims — it verifies mechanical integrity only.
notes.discrepancy_fingerprint: A deterministic hash of the claim evaluation results. Different discrepancy fingerprints indicate different evaluation outcomes. The exact fingerprint values are specimen-specific and may change if claim evaluation logic changes.
notes.what_claim_check_PASS_means: All critical claims in the claim set were satisfied by the evidence. This does NOT mean the evidence is complete, correct, or trustworthy in all contexts — only that the specific claims evaluated were supported by the receipts present.
notes.what_claim_check_FAIL_means: At least one critical claim was NOT satisfied. The discrepancy_fingerprint identifies which combination of claims failed. The verify_report.json contains per-claim details.
